fields.label: Don't think about it. Just deliver it.
Open a corner of the tape.
Count the money in the hallway.
Answer it
fields.arrival_flavor: You pick up. Neither of you says anything for a moment.

fields.label: Let it ring out
fields.arrival_flavor: It goes to voicemail. Twenty seconds later, a text: 'I need help. Please.' You decide to call back.

fields.label: Tell her you'll handle it
fields.arrival_flavor: She hangs up before you can say anything else.

fields.label: Ask her why she called you specifically
fields.arrival_flavor: Silence. Then: 'Because you owe me one. You owe me about a thousand.'

fields.label: Tell her you'll handle it
fields.arrival_flavor: She hangs up. You sit with it for a minute before you move.

fields.label: Talk him down
fields.arrival_flavor: You keep your voice low. Make sure he has to lean in to hear you.

fields.label: Don't waste words
fields.arrival_flavor: You close the distance before he finishes the sentence.

fields.label: Go home
fields.arrival_flavor: The walk back is quiet. That's fine.

fields.label: Check the job board.
fields.arrival_flavor: You head into Sal's back room.

fields.label: Head back outside.
fields.arrival_flavor: You step back out onto the street.

fields.label: Head back outside.
fields.arrival_flavor: You leave Sal's and step back into the cold.

fields.label: Slip around back.
fields.arrival_flavor: You move through the shadows and find an unlocked side door.

fields.label: Take them down.
fields.arrival_flavor: Both men are down. You step over them and into the warehouse.
fields.failure_arrival_flavor: They kick your ass. Quickly and without mercy.

fields.label: Head through the service entrance.
fields.arrival_flavor: The side corridor is quiet. Crates of cigarettes, mostly. Nothing worth dying over.

fields.label: Move toward the voices.
fields.arrival_flavor: You ease around the shelving and find a man alone, going through ledgers.

fields.label: Head back to the corner.
fields.arrival_flavor: You walk back across town, jaw sore, empty-handed.

fields.label: Head back to the corner.
fields.arrival_flavor: You take the long way back, making sure no one's behind you.

fields.label: Head back to the corner.
fields.arrival_flavor: You walk out into the night with leverage.

fields.label: Head back to the corner.
fields.arrival_flavor: You leave the dock lighter than you expected and heavier where it counts.

fields.label: Step to him
fields.arrival_flavor: You cross the street. He tenses.

fields.label: Head back
fields.arrival_flavor: You pocket the envelope and disappear into the crowd.

fields.label: Drag yourself home
fields.arrival_flavor: You take the back streets to avoid anyone seeing you like this.

fields.label: Take his money and go
fields.arrival_flavor: You're three blocks away before he stirs.

fields.label: Take the standard rate and move.
fields.arrival_flavor: You take the case. It's light. Russ lights the cigarette at last.

fields.label: Break your pattern and lose him.
Head back to the corner.
fields.arrival_flavor: You fold the envelope into your jacket and take the long route back.

fields.label: Walk it off.
fields.arrival_flavor: You find a diner three blocks away and sit there until you feel clean.

fields.label: Head back to the corner.
fields.arrival_flavor: You leave Russ counting his cut and head back. Tonight was easy money.

fields.label: Step to him directly.
fields.arrival_flavor: You cross the street. He turns to face you without hurrying.

fields.label: Drag yourself home.
fields.arrival_flavor: You take the back streets. Nobody needs to see this.

fields.label: Back to the square.
fields.arrival_flavor: You leave him with something to think about.

fields.label: Ask where she is
fields.arrival_flavor: She's inside. Has been since last night.

fields.label: Go meet Emilio
fields.arrival_flavor: You pocket the card. Your sister doesn't say goodbye.

fields.label: Sit down
fields.arrival_flavor: You sit. You don't touch the coffee.

fields.label: Make him see it your way
fields.arrival_flavor: Emilio listens. Really listens. Then he nods, once, slowly.
fields.failure_arrival_flavor: Something shifts in his expression. He folds the paper away. Two guys appear from the back.

fields.label: Shake on it
fields.arrival_flavor: His grip is firm. His eyes are already somewhere else.

fields.label: Leave
fields.arrival_flavor: The door closes quietly behind you. No drama. Just business.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: You put the kettle on. Small victories.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: You take the long way home. Old habit. Good one.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: You walk slow. Nothing to rush home to.

fields.label: Take the alley door
fields.arrival_flavor: The lock gives on the third pick. You're in.

fields.label: Move fast — straight out the way you came
fields.arrival_flavor: You move. Box under one arm, back the way you came, into the alley.

fields.label: Wait for the gap and go
fields.arrival_flavor: The light passes. They don't stop. You're three blocks away before you hear the sirens.
fields.failure_arrival_flavor: A second car comes around the other end. Nowhere left to go.

fields.label: Talk your way out
fields.arrival_flavor: You keep your voice level. Wrong street, wrong night, wrong person.

fields.label: Hold the story together
fields.arrival_flavor: Something shifts. They let you go. You don't look back.
fields.failure_arrival_flavor: The story falls apart. One of them reaches for the radio.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: You put the box under the floorboard and sleep better than you have in weeks.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: You take the long way. Keep your head down. The box stays under your jacket.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: Nothing to show for it. You put the kettle on and don't think about what's next.

fields.label: Head to Marek's
fields.arrival_flavor: Ansel Street. The dry cleaner's sign flickers. You push the door open.

fields.label: Agree
fields.arrival_flavor: Marek slides a piece of paper across the counter. Address on it. Nothing else.

fields.label: Make the case
fields.arrival_flavor: He says the debt is three hundred. Says you can keep fifty if you collect it clean.
fields.failure_arrival_flavor: He goes back to his newspaper. The address is still on the counter.

fields.label: Go in during shift change
fields.arrival_flavor: Priya holds the door. Doesn't make eye contact. You're in.

fields.label: Move fast and get out
fields.arrival_flavor: Back on the street with the box under your jacket. Priya doesn't know your name. Good.
fields.failure_arrival_flavor: A door opens at the wrong moment. A uniform, half-awake, holding a coffee.

fields.label: Bluff — wrong room, wrong floor
fields.arrival_flavor: You keep it simple. Tired voice. Wrong floor, sorry, heading back up.

fields.label: Hold the story
fields.arrival_flavor: He points you back toward the stairwell. You thank him. Neither of you will remember this.
fields.failure_arrival_flavor: He reaches for his radio. That's that.

fields.label: Head to Marek's
fields.arrival_flavor: Same dry cleaner's. Same smell. Marek looks up this time.

fields.label: Leave him with a warning
fields.arrival_flavor: He nods from the floor. Says he'll have it by Friday. You believe him.

fields.label: Take the money and go
fields.arrival_flavor: Clean. You didn't even have to raise your voice.

fields.label: Read the room before it closes
fields.arrival_flavor: Something's wrong. You clock it a beat before it becomes a problem.

fields.label: Move first
fields.arrival_flavor: You're already moving before they expect it. The room tips in your favor.
fields.failure_arrival_flavor: Too slow. One of them gets a hand on you.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: The cashbox goes under the floorboard next to the jewels. Not bad for a week's work.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: You walk fast and don't look back. Marek's still out there. So are you.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: Nothing to show for it. Marek has everything. You have a walk home.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: Released at six in the morning. The city is already going without you.

fields.label: Go to the storage facility
fields.arrival_flavor: You go at night. The place is quieter than it should be.

fields.label: Fight your way out
fields.arrival_flavor: Three men, tight quarters, low light. You've had worse.

fields.label: Hold the performance together
fields.arrival_flavor: One of them glances at the other. Just for a second. That's enough.
fields.failure_arrival_flavor: They stop listening. The closest one moves first.

fields.label: Cut her loose and hear her out
fields.arrival_flavor: She works the zip tie off her wrists and doesn't say thank you. You appreciate that.

fields.label: Take out the men and free the woman
fields.arrival_flavor: Fast and clean. The woman is already pulling at her zip ties when you reach her.

fields.label: Take her in
fields.arrival_flavor: She nods. Doesn't smile. You head out.

fields.label: Hold your ground
fields.arrival_flavor: She gives it up. Says she hopes you're better at finishing things than starting them.
fields.failure_arrival_flavor: The information and her come as a package. You can take it or leave it.

fields.label: Hit it now before he's gone
fields.arrival_flavor: No time for a plan. You move.

fields.label: Move into position
fields.arrival_flavor: Marek's cornered before he knows it.
fields.failure_arrival_flavor: He clocks you. By the time you reach the warehouse he's ready.

fields.label: Go in anyway
fields.arrival_flavor: Three on one. You've had worse. You tell yourself that.

fields.label: Move when the moment comes
fields.arrival_flavor: You're behind Marek before his men see you. Clean entry.
fields.failure_arrival_flavor: The moment doesn't come. You go in anyway.

fields.label: Take the name and let him go
fields.arrival_flavor: He says the name. Callum Voss. Import business, clean on paper.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: Sable says she'll be in touch. You believe her.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: Marek's phone has seventeen contacts you don't recognize yet. That's tomorrow's problem.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: Sable walks with you as far as the corner. Doesn't say anything. Doesn't need to.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: You walk it off. The city doesn't care either way.

fields.label: Hear him out
fields.arrival_flavor: You sit down. You don't have a choice and both of you know it.

fields.label: Make the case
fields.arrival_flavor: He shaves the number. Not much. Enough to feel like a win that isn't.
fields.failure_arrival_flavor: He adds interest for the inconvenience.

fields.label: Case the print shop first
fields.arrival_flavor: You spend an afternoon across the street with a coffee.

fields.label: Go in after hours
fields.arrival_flavor: Seven-fifteen. Garrett's light is still on in the back.

fields.label: Crack the keypad
fields.arrival_flavor: Four digits. You try the year on the business license first.

fields.label: Try it
fields.arrival_flavor: It works. The door swings open.
fields.failure_arrival_flavor: Three wrong attempts and a new sound from upstairs. Garrett heard something.

fields.label: Tell him it's Marek
fields.arrival_flavor: Something shifts in his face. Not fear. Recognition.

fields.label: Take both folders and the plates — decide later
fields.arrival_flavor: Garrett watches you weigh it up. He's been here before.

fields.label: Walk out with everything
fields.arrival_flavor: He lets it go. You've got both folders and the plates and a head start.
fields.failure_arrival_flavor: Garrett calls your bluff. He wants a commitment, not a maybe.

fields.label: Take the filing cabinet contents too
fields.arrival_flavor: Garrett's private life, documented in receipts and photographs. Ugly stuff.

fields.label: Deliver to Marek
fields.arrival_flavor: Garrett watches you go. He doesn't try to stop you.

fields.label: Find Sable
fields.arrival_flavor: She's not hard to find if you know what name to ask for.

fields.label: Agree
fields.arrival_flavor: She nods. Doesn't shake your hand. You're already past that.

fields.label: Walk away
fields.arrival_flavor: Marek watches you go. He doesn't say goodbye.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: Sable will be in touch. For the first time in a while, you believe that's a good thing.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: The folder goes somewhere safe. Marek doesn't know it exists. That's the whole advantage.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: You breathe easier on the walk home. Not much. But some.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: Nothing went right. You go home and figure out what's left.

fields.label: Answer it
fields.arrival_flavor: You pick up. Neither of you says anything for a moment.

fields.label: Tell her you'll handle it
fields.arrival_flavor: She hangs up before you can say anything else.

fields.label: Tell her you'll handle it
fields.arrival_flavor: She hangs up. You sit with it for a minute before you move.

fields.label: Talk him down
fields.arrival_flavor: You keep your voice low. Make sure he has to lean in to hear you.

fields.label: Let him think about it
fields.arrival_flavor: Something shifts behind his eyes. He nods, once, and steps back.
fields.failure_arrival_flavor: He laughs. Calls something over his shoulder. Two guys emerge from the alley.

fields.label: Go home
fields.arrival_flavor: The walk back is quiet. That's fine.

fields.label: Go home
fields.arrival_flavor: You take the long way. Let the adrenaline settle before you walk through the door.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: You hang your jacket up. First time in three years that felt normal.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: You ice your ribs over the sink. The city keeps going. So do you.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: You take the long way. Nobody should see you like this.

fields.label: Go to the meeting
fields.arrival_flavor: Word travels. Apparently talking someone down is more impressive than it sounds in this city.

fields.label: Take the job
fields.arrival_flavor: Morris leaves first. You finish his water.

fields.label: Knock
fields.arrival_flavor: Three knocks. The television goes quiet.

fields.label: Make clear this isn't a conversation
fields.arrival_flavor: You step inside without being invited. Close the door behind you.

fields.label: Wait him out
fields.arrival_flavor: Something breaks behind his eyes. He goes to the bedroom. Comes back with an envelope.
fields.failure_arrival_flavor: He straightens up. His voice cracks but he holds the line. He doesn't have it and you can do what you want.

fields.label: Offer him a deal — three hundred now, three hundred by end of month
fields.arrival_flavor: You lay it out like it's a negotiation, not a favour. It's better for him if he believes that.

fields.label: Let him decide
fields.arrival_flavor: He nods. Goes to the bedroom. Comes back with three hundred in a ziplock bag like he's been keeping it ready.
fields.failure_arrival_flavor: He shakes his head. Says he's heard this before. Says the last guy who offered him a deal took the three hundred and sent someone else the next week anyway.

fields.label: Give him one more chance
fields.arrival_flavor: You step closer. Make sure he's looking at you.

fields.label: Head home
fields.arrival_flavor: The envelope sits in your jacket like it weighs more than it does.

fields.label: Head home
fields.arrival_flavor: You hope Pruitt actually sends the rest. You won't follow up. That part's not your problem.

fields.label: Head home
fields.arrival_flavor: The walk back is longer than it should be. You're thinking the whole way.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: Dutch knows your name now. Whether that's good news depends on what you do next.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: Half a job. You'll do better next time. Probably.

fields.label: Back to the apartment
fields.arrival_flavor: You owe Dutch a good job. Get one.

fields.label: Go to Sal's bar.
fields.arrival_flavor: You push through the unmarked door.

fields.label: Go for the crates.
fields.arrival_flavor: The padlock is cheap. The contents are not.

fields.label: Walk away
fields.arrival_flavor: You keep moving.

fields.label: Leave him and go
fields.arrival_flavor: You leave him where he fell.

fields.label: Push for a better cut.
fields.arrival_flavor: Russ's jaw tightens. 'Ten percent is the rate.' You hold his eye.

fields.label: Talk first.
fields.arrival_flavor: He watches you approach. Arms loose at his sides.

fields.label: Tell her not to move
fields.arrival_flavor: She says she knows. She wasn't planning on it.

fields.label: Go through the front window
fields.arrival_flavor: Glass on the mat. Thirty seconds of exposure. Nobody sees.

fields.label: Kill the lights and wait it out
fields.arrival_flavor: Bad call. A patrol car rolls up in two minutes flat.

fields.label: Run for it
fields.arrival_flavor: You don't wait for them to decide.

fields.label: Hit the evidence lockup first
fields.arrival_flavor: The 9th precinct evidence lockup. Basement level. You know someone on intake.

fields.label: Push back on the terms
fields.arrival_flavor: Marek looks up from his newspaper for the first time.

fields.label: Wait and watch the building first
fields.arrival_flavor: An hour on a bench across the street. You count the rotations. There's a gap.

fields.label: Run
fields.arrival_flavor: You don't give him time to make up his mind.

fields.label: Make sure he remembers
fields.arrival_flavor: He'll remember. So will his neighbors.

fields.label: Wait and see what they want
fields.arrival_flavor: Too slow. By the time you move it's already done.

fields.label: Stall — find out what they know
fields.arrival_flavor: You keep your hands loose and your voice calm. Like you expected this.

fields.label: Cut her loose and take the map
fields.arrival_flavor: She picks up the phone before you can. Says she's coming whether you like it or not.

fields.label: Take the information and go alone
fields.arrival_flavor: She reads you immediately. Folds her arms.

fields.label: Cut off his exit first
fields.arrival_flavor: You split up. Sable keeps eyes on Marek while you move to block the van.

fields.label: Wait and find a smarter angle
fields.arrival_flavor: You settle in. Watch the pattern. There's something here.

fields.label: Take the name and don't honor the deal
fields.arrival_flavor: He gives up the name. Then you take the rest anyway.

fields.label: Push back on the number
fields.arrival_flavor: Marek listens with the patience of a man who finds this mildly entertaining.

fields.label: Go straight in
fields.arrival_flavor: No preparation. Sometimes that's cleaner.

fields.label: Find Garrett and make him open it
fields.arrival_flavor: A sound from the back. Garrett's still here.

fields.label: Say nothing
fields.arrival_flavor: He reaches for something under the counter.

fields.label: Take Marek's folder and the plates — stay on task
fields.arrival_flavor: Clean. You take what Marek asked for and leave the rest on the table.

fields.label: Just take the plates
fields.arrival_flavor: You've got what Marek asked for. The rest isn't your business.

fields.label: Take her information and stay independent
fields.arrival_flavor: She says to remember her name when it mattered. You will.

fields.label: Let it ring out
fields.arrival_flavor: It goes to voicemail. Twenty seconds later, a text. I need help. Please.

fields.label: Ask her why she called you specifically
fields.arrival_flavor: Silence. Then she says you owe her one. You owe her about a thousand.

fields.label: Don't waste words
fields.arrival_flavor: You close the distance before he finishes the sentence.

fields.label: Go to the meeting
fields.arrival_flavor: Word travels. Apparently putting someone on the ground outside a Decker Avenue walkup is the kind of CV entry that gets noticed.

fields.label: Slip a note under the door and walk
fields.arrival_flavor: You write — Dutch knows where you live. You have two days. Fold it small. Slide it under. Then you go. But the door opens before you reach the stairs.

fields.label: Ask him what happened
fields.arrival_flavor: He looks like he wasn't expecting that. Neither were you, if you're honest.

fields.label: Walk away
fields.arrival_flavor: You leave him standing in the doorway. Don't look back.

fields.label: Bribe them to look away.
fields.arrival_flavor: You peel off a few bills. They pocket the cash and find somewhere else to be.

fields.label: Ignore the offer — this was never about information
fields.arrival_flavor: You didn't come here for names. Marek knows that now.

fields.label: Take Garrett's folder only — flip on Marek
fields.arrival_flavor: You leave the plates. Garrett nods. Says he'll consider the debt between you settled.

fields.label: Go to the meeting
fields.arrival_flavor: Word travels. Apparently even losing a fight outside a Decker Avenue walkup is enough to get you on someone's radar. Make of that what you will.
